title: Jeremy Fudge | CEO | BAL Immigration Law
content:
## Chief Executive Officer  // Dallas
“Life comes down to loving and serving others. Corporate immigration affords me the opportunity to achieve my purpose in life by loving our people and serving our clients and, in doing so, making a huge difference in the world as we Power Human Achievement.”
As CEO, Jeremy embodies BAL’s mission to make a positive difference in people’s lives in every aspect of his service to the firm. He crafts BAL’s vision and leads the charge to constantly Pursue the Exceptional in its strategies and operations. Texas CEO Magazine recognized Jeremy as one of just 22 CEOs rated “A+” by their employees, representing the top 5% of employee-rated CEOs of large companies in Texas. Additionally, he was named an Outstanding Director by Dallas Business Journal. Jeremy is in The Legal 500 Hall of Fame; he is recognized as an Eminent Practitioner by Chambers and Partners; and he is an Elected Fellow of the American Bar Foundation. He previously has been named as one of the top 20 Most Powerful Corporate Immigration Lawyers in the country by Lawdragon and has been recognized by D CEO's Dallas 500 as one of the Dallas-Fort Worth area's most influential business leaders. Jeremy also serves on the boards of Leadchange, Lead5 and Greater Europe Mission. He is also active at Baylor University, including helping to establish the Baylor Built student-athlete character-formation program as well as serving on the Give Light campaign and NIL Advisory Council.
University of Texas School of Law, J.D., cum laude Baylor University, B.S., Journalism and Political Science, magna cum laude
